codeReview.summary: The feature adds a procedural dungeon framework, portal wiring, minimap support, and focused tests, but it still misses a required dungeon-exclusive set item and returns players to the dungeon portal area instead of the Abyss Rift entrance when exiting a run.

issues.description: The spec requires at least 2 dungeon-exclusive legendary/set items, but the implementation only defines two exclusive legendaries in `DUNGEON_EXCLUSIVE_LEGENDARIES` and no dungeon-exclusive set item at all. This does not satisfy the required 'legendary/set' content mix.

`exitDungeon()` restarts Abyss Rift at `DUNGEON_PORTAL_COL` / `DUNGEON_PORTAL_ROW - 2`, which returns the player to the portal area instead of the Abyss Rift entrance required by the feature ('exiting returns to Abyss Rift'). The same file already defines `ABYSS_ENTRANCE_COL/ROW` for the required entrance location but does not use them here.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker treated loot-table data assertions as sufficient proof of guaranteed boss drops, but this codebase's runtime guarantees are enforced in `LootSystem` / `ZoneScene`, not by `MonsterDefinition.lootTable` alone.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md notes that `MonsterDefinition.lootTable` is mostly descriptive; the new tests in `src/__tests__/DungeonSystem.test.ts:432-459` only assert loot-table contents / helper return values rather than exercising the runtime drop path.

summary: Reviewed the random-dungeons implementation via handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and relevant runtime files. The feature is close, but it fails the spec because it lacks any dungeon-exclusive set item and still exits dungeon runs to the portal area rather than the Abyss Rift entrance.
